title: Origins, importance and layers

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling draft for Origins, importance and layers; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CSS Cascading and Inheritance Level 5; W3C specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Origins, importance and layers
sources.claim: Cascade origin, importance, layer, specificity, scoping proximity, and source order resolve competing declarations in a defined priority sequence.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Cascade origin, importance, layer, specificity, scoping proximity, and source order resolve competing declarations in a defined priority sequence.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A low-specificity rule in a later author layer can beat a highly specific rule in an earlier layer. Your goal is to determine the winning declaration in a layered multi-origin example. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: html document syntax; Rules, declarations and values. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Cascade origin, importance, layer, specificity, scoping proximity, and source order resolve competing declarations in a defined priority sequence. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Transitions and animations have special positions, user important styles protect preferences, and layers reorder author rules before specificity. This draft therefore rejects: Specificity is always the first and strongest cascade factor.

includes: Predict precedence across user-agent, user and author origins, importance and cascade layers.
Determine the winning declaration in a layered multi-origin example.

analogy.example: A layered stage design can model Origins, importance and layers, where rules size, place, and paint existing content.
usefulFor: Building a first mental model for Origins, importance and layers before using exact platform terms
limitations: Physical layers do not reproduce cascade algorithms, intrinsic sizing, writing modes, or user preferences.
